name: restraint — honest Wins+Insight only, no manufactured struggle/offer/ask (HIGH)

input.name: James
post: Shipped a since-last-post window and an in-app cohort feed.

Wins — James shipped two things on router-daybook today. The daily digest now pulls work since his last Router post instead of a fixed midnight-to-midnight day, anchored to the post's own feed timestamp so the window stays correct on whatever machine he runs it on, with a plain seven-day fallback when there is no prior post. He also added an in-app feed: until today, posting ended on a success screen whose only feed affordance launched the Router in an external browser, and now his own posts and the cohort's render in the app itself, inline on the success screen right after posting and in a persistent feed view reachable from the nav.

Insight — The feature turned out to be a rendering and navigation problem, not a backend one. fetchFeed() already returned the cohort feed and the raw entries endpoint already carried his own posts, so the data to show everything in-app was already on hand; the work was matching the existing Scope-view pattern end to end rather than adding new plumbing. The same was true of the window: anchoring to data that already exists — the Router's record of his posts — beat inventing a local checkpoint file that starts empty, because it just works on a fresh install.

feedEntries.content: Tuning a RAG pipeline for our docs search; chunking strategy is killing recall.
Shipped a Stripe billing portal integration today, webhooks were the tricky part.
Training a small classifier to route support tickets, label noise is rough.

input.workLog: router-daybook: since-last-post window anchored to feed entry.timestamp (server-provided), 7-day fallback; in-app feed view (inline + persistent) reusing the Scope-view pattern. Files: transcripts.js, router.js, main.js, app.js. Everything landed cleanly; no real blockers.
